hypothesis: Enhancing aromatic complementarity at position 5 and hydrophobic packing at position 11 will improve SSTR2 selectivity by strengthening interactions with ECL2 (SSTR2-specific) while avoiding off-target SSTR3/5 interactions.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis remains valid: strong SSTR2 binding can be achieved while reducing off-target interactions. However, we must focus on disrupting conserved pocket contacts in SSTR3/SSTR5 to push Δmargin further above 0.

proposed_changes.rationale: The current threshold allows for weak binding candidates. Raising the ddG threshold will filter out weaker binders and retain only the strongest SSTR2 binders.

To improve selectivity, we need to disrupt off-target contacts at non-pharmacophore positions. Focusing mutations at these positions will help reduce off-target binding while preserving key pharmacophore residues.
